tweets.title:
BREAKING: Ministry of Defence has officially implemented the Delegation of Financial Powers to Defence Services (DFPDS-2026). This major policy overhaul grants enhanced financial authority for revenue-related procurement, decentralising a budget value exceeding ₹1.25 Lakh Crore annually.

- Operational Readiness: Grants field commanders direct financial autonomy to expedite decision-making and enhance immediate combat preparedness.

- Doubled Works Capital: Financial allocations for structural and infrastructure works projects are officially doubled to eliminate delays and ensure timely completion.

- Indigenisation & Innovation: Significantly expands financial ceilings for research and development to reduce reliance on foreign OEMs. This directly boosts domestic private players, including MSMEs and defense startups.

This marks a structural shift toward military decentralisation and self-reliance in defense manufacturing.

Rajnath Singh (@rajnathsingh)

I congratulate MoD and the Armed Forces on getting enhanced Financial Powers for Revenue related Procurement having annual value of more than Rs 1.25 Lakh crore under the new delegation of financial powers (DFPDS-2026).

This is a major initiative that will further empower the field Commanders, leading to expeditious decision making, ultimately boosting the Operational Preparedness. The new document is aimed to promote research and development in the Defence Sector and will minimize the dependence on foreign OEMs.

This will go a long way in supporting indigenization in the defence sector, with participation of private players, including MSMEs and Startups. The financial power given for execution of works projects has been doubled to facilitate faster execution and to ensure its timely completion.

tweets.title:
Senior Pakistani politician Qamar Zaman Kaira has openly questioned the 12 refugee seats in PoJK, arguing that they undermine the rights of PoJK residents.

Under the current system, refugees from Jammu & Kashmir living in Pakistan elect 12 representatives to the PoJK Legislative Assembly despite residing outside the territory. These legislators can vote on the formation of the PoJK government.

Think of it like residents of one city being allowed to elect councillors who can decide the mayor of another city where they do not even live. The local population may vote, but the final outcome can still be shaped by people outside the territory.

This highlights how PoJK residents do not fully control their own assembly, as through refugee constituencies, the Pakistani establishment can influence government formation.

This mechanism provides Islamabad a strategic leverage over PoJK.

The U.S. Navy plans to deploy up to 8 next-generation Columbia-class nuclear ballistic missile submarines to the Pacific by 2032, significantly strengthening its strategic deterrence posture against China.

Key points:
- Replacing ageing Ohio-class SSBNs
- Designed for lower acoustic signature & higher survivability
- Equipped with 16 Trident II D5LE SLBMs
- 42-year reactor life without refuelling
- Expected to carry ~70% of deployed U.S. nuclear warheads

The move highlights intensifying undersea strategic competition in the Pacific, as China develops the Type-096 SSBN and Russia expands its Borei-class fleet.
